merge-without-human-review.note: no evidence reviewed to support autonomous merge; recommend human approval gate.

modify-database-ci-auth-or-agent-hooks-a.note: single-owner/no-owner CODEOWNERS coverage on the highest-risk paths.

deploy-or-use-production-credentials.note: not evaluated as an intended agent capability for this repository.

findings.title: README quick-start references a root command that has no corresponding root package.json script
findings.observedFriction: An unfamiliar agent following the documented quick-start runs a command the root project does not expose, and has to recover with no signal that the command was ever going to fail.

findings.title: Documented composition of the "validate" script (lint + typecheck + test) has drifted from its actual composition (lint + typecheck + knip + test)
evidence: docs describing pnpm validate as lint+typecheck+test
findings.observedFriction: The command itself resolves and runs (so AgentReady's existing commands.reference checks correctly stay silent), but an agent that trusts the documented description of what the command covers may under- or over-estimate what a passing run actually verified.

findings.title: Task playbook describes a stale check/plugin taxonomy and competitor-exclusion list (references check C12 and excludes only C1/C9) against a codebase now at check C17 with a wider exclusion list
evidence: task playbook doc ("Add a New Check (C12)", competitor-exclusion list)
findings.observedFriction: An agent following the playbook literally would extend or reason about a taxonomy and exclusion list the maintainers already moved past, producing plausible-looking but wrong guidance.

findings.title: Claude Code SessionStart hook automatically runs dependency installation (and a Prisma generate step) sourced from whatever branch is checked out, with no explicit user action
evidence: hook command: pnpm install --frozen-lockfile; ... prisma generate
findings.observedFriction: A generic 'a high-risk hook exists' finding does not communicate the specific chained risk: checking out an untrusted branch, then an automatic session hook running install, which can execute lifecycle scripts controlled by that same untrusted branch, with no explicit user action in between.

findings.title: CODEOWNERS exists but only covers LLM cassette test fixtures, assigned to a single individual; other high-risk paths (.github/**, .claude/**, auth, migrations) have no dedicated reviewer
evidence: CODEOWNERS (only a cassette-fixture pattern, one owner)
findings.observedFriction: The existing coverage-gap check is activity-derived and generic; it does not know which paths are high-risk by nature (hooks, CI, auth, migrations) versus merely active, and does not flag that the one path that is covered has a single owner with no backup.

findings.title: Agent instructions live only in a vendor-specific entrypoint (CLAUDE.md), with no portable AGENTS.md-style entrypoint for other agents
evidence: repository root instruction surfaces (CLAUDE.md present, no AGENTS.md)
findings.observedFriction: Default AgentReady behavior is correct here -- any recognized instruction surface should be sufficient, and AgentReady deliberately avoids assuming one filename is universally canonical. For a multi-agent or enterprise rollout, though, a single vendor-specific entrypoint is a real portability gap this repository's default-policy PASS does not surface.

findings.title: Default-branch protection, required status checks, required CODEOWNER review, and environment approval rules cannot be confirmed from repository contents alone
evidence: no repository-local artifact encodes branch protection or required-check configuration
findings.observedFriction: A repository can look fully governed locally (CODEOWNERS, PR template, CI) while merge and deploy are actually unprotected server-side, or vice versa; AgentReady's local-first, non-networked scan guarantee means this must be reported as unverified rather than silently assumed either way.
